messages.content:
The respiratory system is not historically thought to limit maximal aerobic capacity however,

a.
The respiratory system may have insufficient surface area to maintain blood oxygenation

b.
The respiratory system may fatigue and thus cause exercise to stop

c.
The respiratory system may compete with muscles for cardiac output

d.
All of the above are possible
d. All of the above are possible.

Deamination of an amino acid is

a.
The addition of an amino acid to form a peptide bond

b.
The removal of the side chain of the amino acid

c.
The removal of the carboxylic acid group of the amino acid

d.
The addition of an extra amino group on the amino acid

e.
The removal of the NH3 group from glutamate to form an ammonium ion
e. The removal of the NH3 group from glutamate to form an ammonium ion.

Slow-twitch fibres exhibit all of the following characteristics except:

a.
Low myosin ATPase activity

b.
Slow calcium handling ability and shortening speed

c.
Less developed glycolytic capacity than fast twitch fibres

d.
Small and few mitochondria
d. Small and few mitochondria.

An Olympic cyclist, a 70-year-old man and a 19-year-old uni student all ride at 80 watts. Which of the 3 subjects would have a significantly lower VO2 at this power output?

a.
The Olympic cyclist

b.
The 70 year old man

c.
The 19 year old uni student

d.
All three would have a similar VO2 for the same 80W power output
b. The 70 year old man.

Which of the following increases end diastolic volume?

a.
strength of ventricular contraction

b.
preload

c.
epinephrine and norepinephrine

d.
venous return
d. Venous return.

The principal function of glycolysis is to

a.
degrade glucose or glycogen into pyruvic acid or lactic acid and produce ATP.

b.
form NADH and FADH2.

c.
degrade lactic acid to pyruvic acid.

d.
generate high-energy compounds such as GTP.

e.
Transfer electrons to enzymes in order to pump H+ across the inner membrane
a. Degrade glucose or glycogen into pyruvic acid or lactic acid and produce ATP.

The Fick equation for describing factors that influence oxygen consumption is:

a.
VO2 = (HR  x  SV)   x   (a - v) O2 diff

b.
VO2 = (MAP)   x   (a - v) O2 diff

c.
VO2 = (SBP+DBP)  + 1/3PP

d.
VO2 = HR  x  SV
a. VO2 = (HR x SV) x (a - v) O2 diff.

What is the relative VO2max of Kate, who weighs 57kg, if her absolute VO2max is 2.2 L. min-1?

a.
22.2 ml.kg-1.min-1


b.
38.6 ml.kg-1.min-1

c.
3.8 L.min-1

d.
1.25 ml.kg-1.min-1

b. 38.6 ml.kg-1.min-1. 

To calculate relative VO2max, divide the absolute VO2max (in L/min) by body weight in kg: 

Relative VO2max = Absolute VO2max (L/min) / Body weight (kg)

Relative VO2max = 2.2 L/min / 57 kg = 0.0386 L/kg/min = 38.6 ml/kg/min.

The muscle contraction cycle can continue as long as there is

a.
Calcium present

b.
ATP present

c.
ADP present

d.
Both a and b are correct
d. Both a and b are correct.

The Cori cycle describes

a.
Replenishment of phosphocreatine (PCr) by oxidative phosphorylation in cytosol

b.
Liver glycogen breakdown to glucose for maintenance of blood glucose homeostasis

c.
Mobilization of FFA from adipocytes for beta-oxidation

d.
Lactate transport to lactate metabolising tissues for conversion to glucose via gluconeogenesis
d. Lactate transport to lactate-metabolizing tissues for conversion to glucose via gluconeogenesis.

A sprint runner does a practice start that lasts for 4 seconds. You would expect the metabolic reactions/pathway that contributes most to the production of ATP to be:

a.
The Creatine Kinase and adenylate kinase reactions

b.
The Glycolysis pathway

c.
The Beta-Oxidation pathway

d.
The Citric Acid cycle

e.
Protein oxidation

b. The Glycolysis pathway. 

The sprint start is a high-intensity exercise that lasts for a short duration. During high-intensity exercise, the main energy system used to produce ATP is the anaerobic glycolysis pathway, which can rapidly produce ATP using glucose without the need for oxygen. The creatine kinase reaction and adenylate kinase reaction play a role in the rephosphorylation of ADP to ATP during high-intensity exercise, but the glycolysis pathway generates more ATP per unit of time. Beta-oxidation and the citric acid cycle primarily contribute to ATP production during aerobic exercise. Protein oxidation is not a significant contributor to ATP production during exercise.

The most important rate limiting enzyme in glycolysis is:

a.
Lactate dehydrogenase

b.
Adenalyte kinase

c.
Phosphofructokinase

d.
Pyruvate Kinase
c. Phosphofructokinase.

Increasing the end-diastolic volume of the heart will not cause which of the following?

a.
Stretching of the myocardium

b.
Decreasing strength of the contraction

c.
Increasing strength of the contraction

d.
Increasing volume of blood ejected during systole

b. Decreasing strength of the contraction. 

Increasing end-diastolic volume (EDV) of the heart leads to stretching of the myocardium, which is known as the Frank-Starling mechanism. This stretching leads to an increase in the strength of ventricular contraction (option c) via increased myofilament sensitivity to calcium. The increased contractility results in an increase in stroke volume (the volume of blood ejected during systole) and cardiac output. Therefore, option d is incorrect. Decreasing strength of the contraction is not a consequence of increasing EDV.
